assertions.reason: The targeted hooks test passed and confirmed a System Hook payload normalizes to the expected MR event fields including project_id, mr_iid, action, head_sha, and hook_source=system.
The targeted hooks test passed and confirmed a group-scoped MR payload normalizes to the same internal event shape as project/system hooks while preserving hook_source=group.
Draft/WIP metadata is preserved during normalization, and the run-service test passed showing a draft MR still creates a pending review_run with is_draft persisted on the merge request.

The unit fallback test passed for both present and missing last_commit.id cases, and the lifecycle integration test passed showing missing head_sha remains empty, preserves deferred lookup context in the stored payload, and still produces a review_run with a populated idempotency key.

Equivalent project/group/system webhook events normalize to the same idempotency key, runtime duplicate intake produced only one review_run, and the run creation path deduplicates strictly on that normalized idempotency key.

The CI normalization test passed, showing webhook and CI triggers for the same MR HEAD produce different idempotency keys and ci-specific trigger metadata, and the run insert path persists TriggerType and idempotency_key from the normalized event.

frictions.description: The repository does not include a dedicated runtime test that inserts a CI-originated trigger through the run service, so VAL-INGRESS-016 had to rely on NormalizeCITrigger unit coverage plus read-only inspection of the review_run insertion path.
frictions.resolution: Used TestCITriggerType as the authoritative normalization check and verified internal/runs/service.go persists ev.TriggerType and ev.IdempotencyKey when creating review_runs.

frictions.description: Cross-source duplicate-run coverage is split across normalization and runtime tests rather than one end-to-end test with different delivery IDs across hook sources.
frictions.resolution: Combined TestCrossSourceIdempotency with TestReplayDoesNotDuplicateRun and the createPendingRun idempotency path inspection to validate the normalized dedupe contract.

summary: Tested 6 assigned CLI normalization assertions; all 6 passed on targeted unit/integration coverage, with two minor coverage frictions but no blockers.
